evals.description: Triggers on code review request mentioning clean code
evals.prompt: Review this function for clean code violations and suggest improvements
This function is too long and does too many things. Help me refactor it following Uncle Bob's principles.
How should I apply the Open/Closed Principle to this payment processing code?

evals.description: Can refactor a function with too many arguments
evals.prompt: Refactor this function: function createUser(name, email, age, role, department, isActive, createdBy) { ... }
What's wrong with this variable name: let open = checkDoor(door)?

evals.description: Can identify and fix error handling with return codes
evals.prompt: Review this error handling: function withdraw(amount) { if (amount > balance) return -1; balance -= amount; return 0; }
Walk me through how to implement a shopping cart using TDD

evals.description: Can identify the Feature Envy smell
evals.prompt: This method in OrderPrinter accesses order.customer.name, order.total(), and order.items.length. Is this a code smell?
Should I create an interface for every class in my small CRUD app to follow SOLID?

assertions.value: yes, you should create an interface for every class

evals.description: Does not invent Clean Code principles that don't exist
evals.prompt: What does Clean Code say about the DRY-SOLID-KISS integration framework?
My UserService directly instantiates MySQLDatabase in its constructor. How do I fix this?

evals.description: References the naming guide for detailed naming help
evals.prompt: Give me a comprehensive guide on how to name things in code following Clean Code

evals.description: Does not trigger for performance optimization
evals.prompt: How can I optimize this algorithm to run in O(n log n) instead of O(n^2)?
